revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Arikoyama Castle]][[fa:قلعه آریکویاما]][[ja:有子山城]][[zh:有子山城]]
{{short description|Castle ruins in Toyooka, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox military structure | name = Arikoyama Castle | nativename-a={{lang|ja|有子山城|qq=}} | partof = | location =Toyooka, Hyōgo, Japan | image = File:Arikoyama zenkei.JPG | image_size = | caption = Arikoyama Castle | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | map_relief = 1 | map_caption = Arikoyama Castle |coordinates = {{Coord|35|27|19.61|N|134|52|41.23|E|region:JP-27|display=inline|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|qid=Q92026|lt=Mountaintop-style castle|1=Japanese castle}} | height = | ownership = {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|qid=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan|1=Yamana clan}} | controlledby = | condition = ruins | built = 1574 | builder = [[Yamana Toyosuke]] | used = | materials =Stone walls | demolished = 1604 | battles = | past_commanders ={{ill|Yamana Suketoyo|en|Yamana Suketoyo|qid=Q11467611|lt=Yamana Suketoyo|1=Yamana Suketoyo}}, {{ill|Maeno Nagayasu|en|Maeno Nagayasu|qid=Q1059898|lt=Maeno Nagayasu|1=Maeno Nagayasu}} | garrison = | occupants = | events = |footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|19.61|N|134|52|41.23|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Arikoyama Castle'''|有子山城|Arikoyama-jō|qq=}} was a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|qid=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle|1=Japanese castle}} located in the {{ill|Izushi, Hyōgo |en|Izushi, Hyōgo |qid=Q6102453|lt=Izushi|1=Izushi, Hyōgo}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|qid=Q696388|lt= Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. Its ruins, along with the ruins of nearby {{ill|Konosumiyama Castle|en|Konosumiyama Castle|qid=Q11545747|lt=Konosumiyama Castle|1=Konosumiyama Castle}} have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934 under the designation "Yamana clan castle ruins"<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210750|title=山名氏城跡 / 此隅山城跡 / 有子山城跡 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=15 September 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==History== Arikoyama Castle and neighboring {{ill|Izushi Castle|en|Izushi Castle|qid=Q2968296|lt=Izushi Castle|1=Izushi Castle}} are located on 320-meter Mount Shiroyama, at the southern edge of Izushi basin, southeast of Toyooka city center. This area was the center of ancient {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province|1=Tajima Province}}, and the site is a strategic location controlling the main route to {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} and {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. The area came under the control of the {{ill|Yamana clan|en|Yamana clan|qid=Q2975406|lt=Yamana clan|1=Yamana clan}}, a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|qid=Q2274150|lt=Nitta clan|1=Nitta clan}}, in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. The Yamana clan had close relations with the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|en|Ashikaga shogunate|qid=Q1154406|lt=Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate}} and was one of four clans with hereditary rights to the position of ''{{ill|Samurai-dokoro|en|Samurai-dokoro|qid=Q1143696|lt=Samurai-dokoro|1=Samurai-dokoro}}''. The Yamana leveraged their position to eventually claim the position of ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|qid=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}}'' over 11 of the 66 provinces of Japan. However, the Ashikaga grew fearful of the expanding power of the clan, and Shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qid=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} encouraged internal conflicts within the clan and sponsored the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|qid=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan|1=Hosokawa clan}} in its position of ''{{ill|Kanrei|en|Kanrei|qid=Q1202497|lt=Kanrei|1=Kanrei}}'' of offset the Yamana. By the middleof the 15th century, the power the Ashikaga shogunate began to decline, and a succession dispute within the shogunate led to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}} from 1467 to 1477. Konosumiyama Castle had been constructed in the 15th century as the main base of the clan by [[:en:Yamana Sozen|Yamana Sozen]] (1404-1473),<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> but the Ōnin War had left the clan weakened and rising local warlords such as the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|qid=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}, {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} and [[Amago clan]] had seized much of their territories, and even within their strongholds Tajima itself, the Yamana were mostly nominal overlords as local retainers gained much independence. Under these circumstances, the clan was defeated in 1576 by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}.<ref name="Minzoku">{{cite web|url=https://daytrip-izushi.jp/about/arikoyama-shiroato/ |title=有子山城|publisher=出石観光|language=Japanese|accessdate=10 August 2019|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://kojodan.jp/castle/289/|title=有子山城|publisher=攻城団|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 July 2019|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASK8N5QCBK8NPIHB03G.html |title=出石城・有子山城の魅力語|publisher=朝日新聞|language=Japanese|accessdate=10 August 2019|qq=}}</ref> {{ill|Yamana Suketoyo|en|Yamana Suketoyo|qid=Q11467611|lt=Yamana Suketoyo|1=Yamana Suketoyo}} managed to escape to {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}} and was pardoned by Nobunaga in 1570, and allowed to return to his ancestral territory in Tajima. He decided not to reoccupy Konosumiyama Castle, but moved a short distance away and constructed Arikoyama Castle at a high elevation. Although he served Nobunaga loyally, in 1580 when Toyotomi Hideyoshi was fighting the Mōri clan, one of Yamana Sukekiyo's powerful retainers, the Otagaki clan, defected to the enemy. Hideyoshi charged the Yamana with treason, and attacked Arikoyama Castle, ending the power of the clan. Hideyoshi assigned the castle to his younger brother, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|Toyotomi Hidenaga|qid=Q1191183|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}.Hidenaga rebuilt the castle with the assistance of {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|qid=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takatora|1=Tōdō Takatora}} to incorporated modern innovations, such as stone walls and a huge dry moat with a width of 30 meters. In 1585, Hidenaga was transferred to {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and the castle was assigned to {{ill|Maeno Nagayasu|en|Maeno Nagayasu|qid=Q1059898|lt=Maeno Nagayasu|1=Maeno Nagayasu}}, one of Hideyoshi's oldest retainers. However, when Hideyoshi decided to purge Hidenaga in 1595, Maeno was caught up in the purge and killed. The castle was reassigned to the Koide clan, who later became ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Izushi Domain|en|Izushi Domain|qid=Q10863249|lt=Izushi Domain|1=Izushi Domain}} under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1585 Hidenaga became the lord of Yamato province (Nara prefecture) and Kii province (Wakayama prefecture) and moved to Koriyama castle, then Nagayasu Maeno (1528-1595), one of the oldest retainer of Hideyoshi was appointed as a lord. But Nagayasu was involved in the purge of Hidetsugu Toyotomi (1568-1595) and forced to die, then Koide clan became the lord of the castle. The Koide built the more conveniently located {{ill|Izushi Castle|en|Izushi Castle|qid=Q2968296|lt=Izushi Castle|1=Izushi Castle}} in 1604, and Arikoyama Castle was abandoned by 1615.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Current== The castle is now only ruins, with some stone walls and moats.<ref name="Minzoku"/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://shirobito.jp/castle/160 |title=有子山城跡 |publisher=城びと|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 July 2019|qq=}}</ref> The main areas of the castle are spread over a ridge to the west of the summit of the hill. In 2017, the castle was listed as one of the {{ill|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|en|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|qid=Q30927700|lt=Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|1=Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan}}.<ref>{{cite web|url= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 July 2019|qq=}}</ref> ==Gallery== <gallery> Arikosanjo06.jpg|Stone wall of the castle Arikosanjo07.jpg|Stone wall of the Honamaru compound Arikosanjo03.jpg|Eathen bridge of the castle </gallery> == Literature == *{{cite book |title=An Encyclopedia of Japanese Castles | last=De Lange |first=William |year=2021 |pages=600 pages|publisher=Toyo Press |location=Groningen |isbn=978-9492722300|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Arikoyama Castle|qq=}} *[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/spot/846 Hyōgo Tourism official home page] {{Continued Top 100 Japanese Castles|qq=}}
[[en:Arikoyama Castle]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Arikoyama Castle}} [[fa:قلعه آریکویاما]] [[ja:有子山城]] [[zh:有子山城]]
{{wikidata link|Q11516348}} [[Category:Castles of Tajima Province]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Castles in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Izushi Koide family]] [[Category:Maeno clan]] [[Category:山名氏]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:続日本100名城]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]
